channel.title: 영문대표홈페이지 > Press > Press Briefings
channel.description: RSS 주소목록 영문대표홈페이지 > Press > Press Briefings

item.title: Spokesperson's Press Briefing (July 14, 2026)
item.content:encoded: Press Briefing Spokesperson and Deputy Minister for Public Affairs Park Il July 14, 2026 14:30 KST Good afternoon. Let me start today’s briefing. Minister of Foreign Affairs Cho Hyun will visit Manila, the Philippines, from July 22 to 23 for the 2026 ASEAN-related Foreign Ministers’ Meetings. On July 22, the Foreign Minister will co-chair with his counterpart from Thailand the 29th ASEAN-ROK Foreign Ministers’ Meeting and have bilateral foreign ministerial meetings with his counterparts from the Philippines, the ASEAN Chair for 2026, and other major member countries of ASEAN. On July 23, Foreign Minister Cho will attend the 27th ASEAN Plus Three Foreign Ministers’ Meeting, followed by the 33rd ASEAN Regional Forum (ARF) Foreign Ministers’ Meeting and the 16th East Asia Summit (EAS) Foreign Ministers’ Meeting. Later in the day, the Foreign Minister will co-chair with his Thai counterpart the 14th ROK-Mekong Foreign Ministers' Meeting. At the upcoming ASEAN-related Foreign Ministers’ Meetings, Foreign Minister Cho will discuss with other participants ways to implement in earnest the Korean government's “ASEAN-Korea CSP (Contributor, Springboard, Partner) Vision” and step up substantive cooperation between Korea and ASEAN in such areas as artificial intelligence (AI) as well as cultural and creative industries. Foreign Minister Cho, together with his Chinese and Japanese counterparts, will seek ways for the ASEAN Plus Three platform to help strengthen the resilience of supply chains for energy and food, among others, in the region. He will also convey the Korean government's commitment to contribute to meeting security demands in the region, including for response to transnational crimes. In addition, the Foreign Minister will ask for ASEAN’s consistent support for the Korean government’s policy on peaceful coexistence and shared prosperity on the Korean Peninsula. (A question-and-answer session followed.)

item.title: Spokesperson's Press Briefing (July 9, 2026)
item.content:encoded: Press Briefing Deputy Spokesperson Lee Moonbae July 9, 2026 14:30 KST Good afternoon. Let me start today’s briefing. After accompanying the President during his participation in the NATO Summit, Minister of Foreign Affairs Cho Hyun will accompany the President on his state visit to Mongolia from today through Saturday, July 11. (A question-and-answer session followed.)

item.title: Spokesperson's Press Briefing (July 7, 2026)
item.content:encoded: Press Briefing Deputy Spokesperson Lee Moonbae July 7, 2026 14:30 KST Good afternoon. Let me start today’s briefing. Minister of Foreign Affairs Cho Hyun will hold an ROK-U.S.-Japan foreign ministers’ meeting with U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Minister for Foreign Affairs of Japan Motegi Toshimitsu in Türkiye on the late afternoon of July 7, local time, on the sidelines of the 2026 North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) Summit. At their first trilateral meeting of the year, the three foreign ministers will discuss Korean Peninsula issues, regional and global affairs, and security and economic cooperation among the three countries. (A question-and-answer session followed.)
